method.closureRule: Every retained causal leg must be present in one verified registered observation per work. Proposal prose, source-adjacent knowledge, and thematic similarity do not close a leg.

retainedPrinciple.finding: The narrowed clue-to-bounded-test-to-success-feedback principle is closed by two distinct verified observations. Breath of the Wild records an optional photograph clue, a location-matching test, and a recovered-memory confirmation. Skyward Sword records an optional surface activation, a map or visual signal that bounds the linked aerial target, and a chest response that confirms the cross-region relation. The latter supports the principle through the post-activation signal, not through an unregistered claim that the surface object is visually distinctive.

closedLegs: bounded signaled island or chest target

narrowing.finding: The specialization remains callable as an optional clue-action-confirmation pattern and no longer generalizes from generic hidden routes or mandatory capability gates.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: The activation scene and registered map marker or visual signal identify the linked aerial destination as a finite target.
exactRequiredEdits.reason: The registry does not state that the surface object is visually distinctive.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: Optional content is readable when at least one observable clue supports a bounded player test and successful testing gives unmistakable feedback that confirms the same clue-action relation; abstain when discovery requires blind exhaustive testing or when the route is required.

Require optionalGoalRef with a passing skip-route assertion, at least one registered clue channel, a bounded target set, an available testActionRef, and a successFeedbackRef. Do not require two cue channels for provenance closure.
Bind optionalGoalRef, clueChannelRef, testActionRef, boundedTargetSetRef, and successFeedbackRef; keep cue redundancy as an accessibility requirement rather than an evidence invariant.

Add required-route-when-skipped, bounded-target, success-identifies-tested-target, no-walkthrough-hint, and blind-exhaustive-search-abstention assertions; lower distinctCueChannels from two to one and do not require a three-way wrong-action/incomplete/success taxonomy as provenance.

retainedPrinciple.finding: Only the Twilight Princess observation closes the proposed shared-fact chain. The Oracle of Ages observation records era-specific scenery and actor placement as context, then a separate item, information, or quest-state interaction that updates dialogue and a clue, reward, or route. It does not record one authoritative fact driving both a visible world-state delta and the NPC delta.

closedLegs: consistent renewed interaction and route feedback

missingLegs: one fact shared by the map and NPC reads
a visible map-state delta caused by the same transaction that updates the NPC
evidence that the local quest condition governs the era scenery

narrowing.finding: The proposed shared-fact consistency specialization removes unsupported notice, personal-stakes, and mandatory player-causation claims and remains useful, but it cannot be approved with the submitted Oracle of Ages edge.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: Use observation.ocarina-of-time.era-shift-rewrites-familiar-routes from work.ocarina-of-time, version.ocarina-of-time.original, and source.game.ocarina-of-time. Bind the active era as the shared fact; bind lighting, occupancy, geometry, music, and paths as the visible world delta; bind residents, dialogue, and actor availability as the NPC delta.
exactRequiredEdits.reason: This already registered observation closes the shared-state comparison; the submitted Oracle of Ages observation does not.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: Use one authoritative world fact to drive both a visible world-state delta and at least one situated NPC dialogue, availability, behavior, service, or access delta, so character response cannot contradict the state the player can observe.

Require one shared worldFactRef and beforeNpcState/afterNpcState with at least one observable NPC delta. Remove mandatory knowledge-transition provenance, named interpretation, personal stakes, and voluntary post-change conversation requirements.

Replace the mandatory update-knowledge, perform-reaction, and social-consequence sequence with a shared-fact read that updates map state and at least one NPC dialogue, placement, schedule, service, tactic, relationship, or access state.
Add shared-fact identity, before/after NPC delta, map-NPC consistency, save/resume, and rollback assertions; remove knowledgeProvenanceValid as a universal hard requirement.

retainedPrinciple.finding: The proposed principle still requires a bounded return-to-control cue and an immediate test of a changed affordance. Neither cited observation records time to control, a first returned-control position, or an immediate affordance test without another transaction. The observations support visible completed restoration and later changed play, not the submitted immediacy claim.

closedLegs: coordinated presentation, inhabitant, service, movement, and route deltas
resolution cue and restored world, narrative, and navigation opportunities
missingLegs: immediate conversation, reward, route, or affordance test
proof that all listed subsystems commit against one registered fact identity

narrowing.finding: Removing mandatory major-reveal, actor-tactic, and player-origin claims is directionally correct, but retaining immediate returned agency still overclaims the registry. A further narrowing can preserve a callable atomic world-change-legibility pattern.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: Treat a player-completed world-state transition as one atomic change: stage a coordinated before-and-after cue and make at least one affected navigation, actor, or interaction state observable in subsequent play.
exactRequiredEdits.reason: The existing observations establish coherent after-state consequences but not immediate control return or an immediate test.

exactRequiredEdits.replacement: Use postTransitionConsequence and quote only the registered later inhabitants, services, conversations, rewards, routes, or exploration opportunities; remove the word immediate.

Treat a player-completed world-state transition as one atomic change: stage a coordinated before-and-after cue and make at least one affected navigation, actor, or interaction state observable in subsequent play.

Remove carriesProtectedReveal, actorTacticChangeCount, and secondsToMeaningfulControl as universal selection requirements. Require a completed transition, defined before/after state, coordinated performance cue, and at least one observable affected-state consequence.

Bind transitionFactRef, beforeVariantRef, afterVariantRef, performanceCueRef, and changedStateRef. Make affected actor state conditional. Remove revealFactRef, mandatory actor-tactic postconditions, and immediate followup-window preconditions unless new observations close them.

Keep atomic commit, rollback, skip/save parity, and post-transition reachability. Remove actorTacticChangeCount >= 1 and secondsToMeaningfulControl <= 20 as evidence-derived hard requirements unless two new registered observations close those legs.

blockers: pattern.npc-reacts-to-world-state: observation.oracle-of-ages.era-specific-resident-updates-quest does not bind one authoritative fact to both a visible map-state delta and the NPC delta; the proposal has only one closed direct-support work.

pattern.embodied-world-change-reveal: neither cited observation records bounded return to control plus an immediate changed-affordance test, so the retained principle and both direct-support edges remain causally open.

The current maturity contracts still contain the broader unsupported cue-count, psychological-reaction, protected-reveal, mandatory-actor-tactic, and immediacy requirements identified in the per-pattern exactRequiredEdits.
